skill.name: red-team-tactics
skill.description: Red team tactics principles based on MITRE ATT&CK framework for authorized adversary simulation and defensive security training.
skill.summary: Educational guide to adversary tactics and techniques for authorized red team operations and security assessment.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged documentation text as malicious patterns, but all findings are false positives. The skill contains educational content about the MITRE ATT&CK framework with no executable code. C2 keywords and reconnaissance references appear only in tables and diagrams explaining adversary tactics for defensive security training purposes.

content.user_title: Learn Red Team Tactics with MITRE ATT&CK Framework
content.value_statement: Security teams need to understand adversary behavior to build effective defenses. This skill provides structured guidance on red team operations based on the industry-standard MITRE ATT&CK framework.
actual_capabilities: Explain MITRE ATT&CK attack phases and their objectives
Describe reconnaissance techniques for authorized assessments
Identify privilege escalation opportunities in Windows and Linux environments
Outline defense evasion techniques for detection testing
Document lateral movement and Active Directory attack patterns
Generate red team reports with detection gap analysis
limitations: Educational content only - does not execute actual attacks
Requires authorized scope and written permission for real engagements
Does not replace hands-on penetration testing tools
Framework knowledge requires practical experience to apply effectively

use_cases.title: Security Assessment Planning
use_cases.description: Plan authorized red team engagements using structured adversary tactics and techniques from MITRE ATT&CK.

use_cases.title: Blue Team Training
use_cases.description: Train defensive security teams on adversary behaviors to improve detection and response capabilities.

use_cases.title: Security Control Validation
use_cases.description: Map existing security controls against adversary techniques to identify coverage gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Attack Phase Explanation
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain the reconnaissance phase of a red team engagement. What are the key objectives and what information should be gathered?

prompt_templates.title: Technique Selection Guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to test detection for credential dumping on Windows. What MITRE ATT&CK techniques should I consider and what are the trade-offs?

prompt_templates.title: Defense Evasion Planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan defense evasion techniques for an authorized assessment. What methods can test detection capabilities while minimizing operational impact?

prompt_templates.title: Report Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a red team report section documenting the lateral movement techniques used, including MITRE ATT&CK IDs, detection gaps identified, and remediation recommendations.

output_examples.input: What are common privilege escalation techniques on Linux systems?
output_examples.output: Common Linux privilege escalation vectors include: SUID binary exploitation (execute files with owner permissions), sudo misconfigurations (NOPASSWD entries, wildcards in allowed commands), kernel vulnerabilities (Dirty Cow, PwnKit), and writable cron jobs (scheduled tasks executed as root). Always verify scope authorization before testing.

output_examples.input: How should I document detection gaps in my red team report?
output_examples.output: For each successful technique, document: (1) The MITRE ATT&CK technique ID and name, (2) Expected detection mechanisms that should have alerted, (3) Root cause analysis for why detection failed, (4) Specific recommendations to improve detection coverage, (5) Priority level based on technique prevalence and impact.

best_practices: Always obtain written authorization and clearly defined scope before any testing
Follow the rules of engagement and minimize impact on production systems
Document all actions taken during the engagement for accurate reporting
anti_patterns: Rushing to exploitation without proper reconnaissance and planning
Causing unnecessary damage or disruption beyond proof-of-concept
Failing to document techniques and skipping detailed reporting

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for learning ethical hacking?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill provides educational content about adversary tactics for authorized security testing. Always practice only on systems you own or have explicit written permission to test.

faq.question: What is the MITRE ATT&CK framework?
faq.answer: MITRE ATT&CK is a globally-accessible knowledge base of adversary tactics and techniques based on real-world observations. It provides a standardized taxonomy for describing adversary behavior during engagements.

faq.question: Can I use this for unauthorized penetration testing?
faq.answer: No. This skill is intended for authorized security assessments only. Unauthorized access to computer systems is illegal. Always obtain proper authorization before conducting any security testing.

faq.question: How does red teaming differ from penetration testing?
faq.answer: Penetration testing focuses on finding vulnerabilities in specific systems. Red teaming simulates full adversary campaigns to test detection and response capabilities across the entire organization.

faq.question: What tools do I need to perform red team operations?
faq.answer: This skill provides tactical guidance and methodology. Actual tooling depends on your engagement scope and may include commercial platforms, open-source tools, or custom scripts. Always ensure tools are used within legal authorization.

faq.question: How do I get started with red teaming as a career?
faq.answer: Build foundational knowledge in networking, operating systems, and security concepts. Pursue relevant certifications (OSCP, CRTO). Practice in legal lab environments. Start with defensive roles to understand detection mechanisms before transitioning to offensive security.
